greeting.say: Afternoon. Gate's bolted, before you ask.

look.text: Bare now, and better for it.
One perfect quince, exactly one arm's length past the top of your reach.

use.text: Nothing left up there but leaves.
You jump for it. Not even close.

itemUse.text: You coax the branch down with the hook. The quince drops into your hand.

look.text: An old quince tree, all knuckles and blossom-scars. The low fruit is long since picked.

use.text: You shake the trunk. One leaf commits.

look.text: A box hedge, barbered to spirit-level flatness.

use.text: You pat the hedge. Firm as a mattress in a strict hotel.

look.text: A wooden handle, deep in the clipped green.

take.text: You drag a pruning hook out of the hedge, over the hedge's loud objection.

look.text: A stout wooden gate in the wall, bolted from the far side.

use.text: You rattle it. The bolt doesn't care.

look.text: Straw hat, green apron, the patience of a slow season.

talk.speaker: target
talk.text: Mind the ruts in the lane. And come back when the medlars turn.

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: Ah — a proper one. Fair's fair.

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: That's mine, by rights. Keep it while you need it.

exits.name: garden gate
exits.look: The gate stands open on a green lane.
